title: Today is the Day for Goodbye What happens to Asuka
display_title: Today is the Day for Goodbye What happens to Asuka
wikitext:
[]: Today is the Day for Goodbye-What happens to Asuka?             Quote Post by tehprognoob » Sat Dec 25, 2010 7:44 pm (Thanks for the song's title, btw, Ornette) I saw that Asuka's voice actor was singing it. Was it possible that the song shows that Asuka was aware of her predicament, and, having just realized her need for people, is sadly saying goodbye to Shinji? The words point to it, and she indeed does meet Shinji again in Q. If you remember when she was in the elevator to Unit 03, she made that heart-wrenching (for an Asuka otaku like me) phone call to Misato (that made her so much more alive as a character for me). From what could be seen, she was so serene and happy. If she'd already settled her differences with herself, then how do you guys think her attitude towards life, other people and especially Shinji will change? As an Asuka fanatic my heart was trembling and about to stroke while the song played. It simply struck me as being so goddamn powerful and touching. Maybe Shikinami isn't all that bad...but back on topic, yea, since she returns in Q...any possibility that Rei will turn out to be Yui incarnate, and Asuka and Rei otakus can have a peace settlement at last? No matter what, that was a powerful song, but I'm just digging for A/S easter eggs in Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Dec 25, 2010 8:33 pm That was actually Rei's voice actress, Megumi Hayashibara, singing both songs. >_> The song is a ye olde one that Anno reused. There's plenty of topics already about this.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Dec 26, 2010 7:34 am So not all S/A-Fans hated rebuild. Yeah, the Bardiel Incident was even more evil than in the original (and they were originally going to do even MORE evil stuff with the poor girl...) Just when she had and Epiphany of sorts, she gets crushed - sort of reminds me of EoE... The dissonant music does a lot of the tricks - many complain that unfitting music was overused, but the Bardiel scene was the only one I can think of - The actual "battle" part of the finale had a remixed version of the Shogoki Theme playing - The "cute" music only started when the GF or if you will, little-sister-saving began. If Asuka returns in Q, they must have some plot in store for her - but I don't think there's going to be S/A. It's not just the shipper in me speaking - except for the bed scene, there was not a single romantic interaction between Re!Shinji and Asuka. (tough there were plenty where we see Asuka herself observing him/getting jealous/trying to win his <3) She WAS shown to be important to him ( "You need to lose someone important, too, father!") but there was never any indication that he views her as anything but a valuable friend. (Or well, in all fairness, they DID keep the "married couple" scene...) They MIGHT have introduced the pairing in Q, but after reading the most recently translated interwiev where the creators said that after laying out the story for the Rebuild movies, they weren't sure what role/function to give to Asuka - I doubt they'd say that if she was going to be part of the film's "offiicial couple"... Of course, there's always a chance that they change their minds before Final is out, but... It'd be a little to late to have the protagonist fall in love with his "official love intrest" in the third installment of a tetralogy. EoE has pretty much a S/A ending with their relationship being pretty much the main focus - I guess they decided it's the R/S-fans' turn in Rebuild...

[]:             Quote Post by tehprognoob » Sun Dec 26, 2010 9:58 am TMBounty_Hunter wrote: That was actually Rei's voice actress, Megumi Hayashibara, singing both songs. >_> The song is a ye olde one that Anno reused. There's plenty of topics already about this.

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Sun Dec 26, 2010 2:25 pm I wouldn't say people dislike Rebuild just because of the shipping aspect. And I also don't think the main goal of Evangelion's story is for the protagonist to fall in love with one of the few girls in the series. Actually I don't think any of the endings (EoTV, EoE and each 1.11, 2.22 ending) so far have been about love or "GF saving" as Kendrix kind of put it. It's just friendship. Shinji just didn't want to lose anybody else in 2.22. And the music in 2.2 was hardly overused, nor cute. The English lyrics to those songs kind of have a meaning. Today is the Day for Goodbye especially. It's kind of messed up. I don't know what I'm saying anymore so I'll sum it up. I do not think Anno's intention is to have a shipping specific ending. I don't think it was since he started Evangelion. Rambleramble.

[]:             Quote Post by tehprognoob » Sun Dec 26, 2010 2:44 pm aye, aye, but fappinghappy fans demand a clear ship

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Dec 26, 2010 3:53 pm I'm not saying the shipping is the most important part of Eva - but the shipping was what the author of the thread asked about. IF there will be shippy end, it won't be A/S, tough... Plus the character's themselves talk about/call it love - Misato suspect love as the cause for Rei changing and Asuka explicitly interprets Rei's talk of feeling "warm and content" as love. There is even one dropped scene that had Rei confessing her feeling to Shinji accompained by the GNR and EVA 01 in a... slightly disturbing Pose. They obviously dropped it cuz it's way to early for both the confession and the... disturbing stuff. I personally found Tsubasa kudasai cute - I am still aware of the meaning and I never claimed they were overused - I just heared a lot of ppl complain about double use of sountrack dissonace and wanted to say that there's actually only one.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sun Dec 26, 2010 6:44 pm So what if Mari or Misato are presented as contenders for romancing Shinji? Misato has always been tenuous at best, but neither storyline has shown her able to clearly define her roles as surrogate mother, commanding officer, big sister, and love interest to Shinji. The main support, however, is shown in the watermelon scene between Shinji and Kaji. Kaji (quite mysteriously) asks Shinji to protect Misato, because he "can't do it himself any more". While superficially, he could just be admitting that he has no business stepping into a fight with an Angel, I am pretty sure that Anno intended a second layer of meaning here. Plus, Kaji's devotion to growing watermelons is a symbol for his caring for Misato. (Note that in NGE, Kaji convinced Shinji to pilot again while watering his watermelons, with the dirty pun that he "would rather be watering Misato's watermelons" at that time.) Also, the 10th Angel fight destroyed the watermelon field, which could signify a falling out between Kaji and Misato, with his possible influence in Mari's hijacking Unit-02 a possible reason. If Shinji ever starts growing watermelons in some of the later movies, Misato should be considered a love interest. And as for Mari, it would be the most objectionable thing a writer could do, by inserting a Mary Sue character as the main protagonists' love interest, even if Mari is intended as a parody of the Mary Sue character phenomenon. Still, Shinji had an intimate encounter with Mari's bosom in 2.0, and she has proven herself as a non-judgmental ally to him during the final battle, so Shinji could perhaps consider her a friend. Now, NGE-Shinji has always had trouble distinguishing between friendship and love, due to his confusion at mixed signals. Rebuild-Shinji has shown no great strength in these issues either, but appears to have a better control of his libido so far. I expect that Mari may be placed into more (quasi-)sexual or (quasi-)romantic situations with Shinji, but this will either be mere fanservice for comedic effect, or a tragic setup, an undermining of her strength leading up to her ultimate downfall. (This is Evangelion we are talking about, after all.) Ultimately, though, Asuka is out. She has officially given up on romancing Shinji. While I expect to see her return for a big shining-moment-of-awesomeness battle to show her character growth (which is probably still to be continued), I don't expect her to play a part in a future romance arc, unless circumstances forcibly remove all contenders and Shinji is forcibly pushed into her direction. Such as in the very last scene of EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Dec 27, 2010 10:33 am I expect Mari to sort of fall for Shinji, but making HER Shinji's main love intrest would be even harder than with Asuka. (she was just introduced, any relationship between them would have to start developing in the third part of a tetralogy -> That just doesn't work) Judging by the little interaction they had untill now, Mari will most likely become some sort of advisor/big sister to Shinj, with some semi-romantic undercurrent, but not much more - don't forget that they also have Kaworu to develop in the next movie. And the Misato/Shinji thing was always somewhat below the surface - she's twice his age it's not like they could actually end up together. We might or might not see the kiss in final, tough I tend towards the latter since we didn't even get the A/S kiss.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Dec 27, 2010 11:25 am Kendrix wrote: And the Misato/Shinji thing was always somewhat below the surface - she's twice his age it's not like they could actually end up together.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Dec 27, 2010 4:43 pm Shinji/Kaji One True Rebuild Ship. There's canon evidence.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Dec 28, 2010 2:45 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: Ultimately, though, Asuka is out. She has officially given up on romancing Shinji. While I expect to see her return for a big shining-moment-of-awesomeness battle to show her character growth (which is probably still to be continued), I don't expect her to play a part in a future romance arc, unless circumstances forcibly remove all contenders and Shinji is forcibly pushed into her direction. Such as in the very last scene of EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Dec 28, 2010 3:22 pm Rei's death is telegraphed, Mari has barely met him, and Misato is nearly three times his age. Asuka is arguably still the only real contender, but it'll never happen because it'd require Khara to end her train of misery.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Dec 28, 2010 4:27 pm esselfortium wrote: Misato is nearly three times his age

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Dec 28, 2010 4:35 pm esselfortium wrote: Rei's death is telegraphed, Mari has barely met him, and Misato is nearly three times his age. Asuka is arguably still the only real contender, but it'll never happen because it'd require Khara to end her train of misery.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Dec 28, 2010 5:28 pm esselfortium wrote: Mari has barely met him

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Dec 28, 2010 5:55 pm Azathoth wrote: Mari has eaten Asuka's role as the quest-for-adulthood, it'll surely be her if it's anyone. Shikinami's theme is reaching out to others and being your true self (or maybe not being your true self; the removal of the facepeeling scene makes it difficult to be sure) - which is going to make any ship a bit of a stretch given that Shikinami's true self is a prancing emo and her false self is a simpering child. Reaching out to others, sure, why the hell not - although I have to wonder why you should dedicate a character to that theme in a series that is entirely about said theme. But sure, why not. Let her reach out to Kaworu or Mari or whoever. Hell, how about she kills Kaworu? Maybe it'll even reveal some interesting aspect of her character. Actually that was a good idea I just came up with, Asuka interacting with Mari. Shikinami could stand to gain a little from the quest-for-adulthood, and Mari is the sort of person she'd get all hot and bothered about (or at least she contains the features of a few such persons).

[]:             Quote Post by tehprognoob » Tue Dec 28, 2010 6:11 pm right. sometimes i forget that the show's about Shinji, lol he does hurt Asuka a lot in either continuity, mostly by running from everything. i believe that fighting zeurel to get Rei back so he doesn't lose more important people also shows his maturity. and that scene, when he stands up from unit 02's hand, his jawline was magnificent

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Tue Dec 28, 2010 8:48 pm Bagheera wrote: Nonsense on all counts. There's plenty of groundwork for Asuka being "in" (particularly if her bit speech to Misato was an act of denial, which seems obvious for many reasons), and whether or not she's "given up" depends an awful lot on what happens in Q (and let's not forget that it's still possible Rebuild is a sequel to NGE; I really hope it's not, but if it is all notion of Asuka being "out" goes out the window).

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Tue Dec 28, 2010 8:57 pm tehprognoob wrote: right. sometimes i forget that the show's about Shinji, lol he does hurt Asuka a lot in either continuity, mostly by running from everything. i believe that fighting zeurel to get Rei back so he doesn't lose more important people also shows his maturity. and that scene, when he stands up from unit 02's hand, his jawline was magnificent
